title: Writing: Giving Directions
learningGoal: Construct sentences using postpositions to state locations, and write polite commands using the formal '-iye' suffix.
instructions: In this writing task, you will play the role of a host directing a formal guest (using 'Aap'). You will tell them where things are and politely ask them to do things.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi:
1. Please come inside. (Come = Aa)
2. Water is on the table.
3. Please drink water. (Drink = Pee)
4. Food is in the house.
promptGoal: Applying formal imperatives and correct postposition word order.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: अंदर आइये। पानी मेज़ पर है। पानी पीजिये। खाना घर में है।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Andar aaiye. Paani mez par hai. Paani pijiye. Khaana ghar mein hai.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Please come inside. Water is on the table. Please drink water. Food is in the house.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect translation. Uses the polite 'iye' form for commands, and correctly places postpositions after the nouns.

evaluationCriteria.description: Places the verb at the very end of the sentence.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: अंदर आइये। पानी मेज़ पर है। पानी पीजिये। खाना घर में है।
acceptableAlternates: Andar aaiye. Paani mez par hai. Paani pijiye. Khaana ghar mein hai.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict checking of postposition word order.

criteria.name: Postposition Reversal Check
criteria.description: Must not write 'par mez' or 'mein ghar'. It MUST be 'mez par' and 'ghar mein'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Flawless! You successfully wrote polite commands and located objects.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review Section 4 regarding postpositions. They must come AFTER the noun they modify, not before.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Don't forget that Peena (to drink) is slightly irregular in the formal command: it becomes 'pijiye'.

hintText: Read the 'Reference Hints' for the exact literal word order in Hindi. Do not use English word order.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Par mez hai water' (using English word order).
